overview: A microservices architecture consisting of a React frontend web application and a Go-based backend API service. The frontend provides employee and manager interfaces for leave management, while the backend handles business logic, data persistence, and RESTful API endpoints. Communication occurs via HTTP/JSON between the web app and the leave-management service.
requirements: Employees must be able to submit leave requests with date range and reason
Managers must be able to view pending leave requests from their direct reports
Managers must be able to approve or reject leave requests
Employees must be able to view the status of their submitted leave requests
All users must be able to view their remaining leave balance
System must distinguish between employee and manager roles
System must track reporting relationships to route requests to appropriate managers
Leave requests must capture start date, end date, and reason
System must maintain leave balance per employee
System must update leave balance upon approval of requests

components.name: leave-web-app
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Web App
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Frontend application for leave management
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Application is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a React-based single-page application using TypeScript and Vite as the build tool. The app should run on port 3000 in development and serve static files in production. Create the following pages and components: (1) Login page with employee ID input to simulate authentication; (2) Employee Dashboard showing leave balance, a form to submit new leave requests (date range picker, textarea for reason, submit button), and a list of submitted requests with status badges (pending/approved/rejected); (3) Manager Dashboard showing pending requests from direct reports with approve/reject buttons, and the manager's own leave balance and requests. Use React Router for navigation. Store the current user role (employee/manager) and ID in React context after login. Make HTTP requests to the leave-management-service backend API at http://leave-management-service:9090 using fetch or axios. Handle loading states and error messages gracefully. Use a component library like Material-UI or Ant Design for consistent styling. The /health endpoint should return {status: 'ok'} with 200 status. In the Dockerfile, use node:18-alpine as base, copy package files, run npm install, build the production bundle with npm run build, then use nginx:alpine to serve the static files from /usr/share/nginx/html. Configure nginx to route all requests to index.html for client-side routing. Expose port 80.

components.name: leave-management-service
components.openAPISpec:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Leave Management Service API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Backend service for managing leave requests and balances
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:9090
paths:
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check endpoint
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /api/employees/{employeeId}/balance:
    get:
      summary: Get leave balance for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Leave balance retrieved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  employeeId:
                    type: string
                  remainingDays:
                    type: integer
        '404':
          description: Employee not found
  /api/employees/{employeeId}/requests:
    get:
      summary: Get all leave requests for an employee
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of leave requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
    post:
      summary: Submit a new leave request
      parameters:
        - name: employeeId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - startDate
                - endDate
                - reason
              properties:
                startDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                endDate:
                  type: string
                  format: date
                reason:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '201':
          description: Leave request created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '400':
          description: Invalid request data
  /api/managers/{managerId}/pending-requests:
    get:
      summary: Get pending leave requests for a manager's direct reports
      parameters:
        - name: managerId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: List of pending requests
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: array
                items:
                  $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
  /api/requests/{requestId}/approve:
    post:
      summary: Approve a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: requestId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request approved
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Request not found
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized
  /api/requests/{requestId}/reject:
    post:
      summary: Reject a leave request
      parameters:
        - name: requestId
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              required:
                - managerId
              properties:
                managerId:
                  type: string
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Request rejected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: '#/components/schemas/LeaveRequest'
        '404':
          description: Request not found
        '403':
          description: Manager not authorized
components:
  schemas:
    LeaveRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
        employeeId:
          type: string
        employeeName:
          type: string
        startDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        endDate:
          type: string
          format: date
        reason:
          type: string
        status:
          type: string
          enum: [pending, approved, rejected]
        requestedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedAt:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        reviewedBy:
          type: string
components.componentAgentInstructions: Implement a Go HTTP service using net/http that listens on port 9090. Use an in-memory data structure (maps and slices) to store employees, leave balances, leave requests, and manager-employee relationships. Initialize with sample data: at least 5 employees (IDs: E001, E002, E003, E004, E005), 2 managers (IDs: M001, M002), each employee starts with 20 days of leave, and assign employees to managers (E001-E003 report to M001, E004-E005 report to M002). Managers also have leave balances. Implement all API endpoints as defined in the OpenAPI spec. For GET /health return {"status":"ok"} with 200. For GET /api/employees/{employeeId}/balance, look up the employee and return their remaining days; return 404 if not found. For GET /api/employees/{employeeId}/requests, return all leave requests for that employee sorted by requestedAt descending. For POST /api/employees/{employeeId}/requests, validate that startDate is before endDate, calculate the number of business days requested (excluding weekends), check if employee has sufficient balance, create a new request with status 'pending' and a unique ID (use UUID or timestamp-based), store it, and return 201 with the created request; return 400 for validation errors. For GET /api/managers/{managerId}/pending-requests, find all employees who report to this manager, collect all their pending requests, and return the list. For POST /api/requests/{requestId}/approve, verify the managerId is the manager of the employee who made the request, change status to 'approved', set reviewedAt to current time, set reviewedBy to managerId, deduct the days from the employee's balance, and return the updated request; return 403 if manager is not authorized, 404 if request not found. For POST /api/requests/{requestId}/reject, same authorization check, change status to 'rejected', set reviewedAt and reviewedBy, do NOT deduct balance, return updated request. Use proper HTTP status codes. Enable CORS to allow requests from the frontend. Structure the code with main.go as entry point, handlers in a handlers package, and data models in a models package. In the Dockerfile, use golang:1.21-alpine as builder, copy go.mod and go.sum, run go mod download, copy source code, build a static binary with CGO_ENABLED=0, then use alpine:latest as runtime, copy the binary, expose port 9090, and run the service. Include logging for each request with method, path, and status code.
